questions.question: What does the particulate nature of matter mean?

options.A: Matter is made of large blocks
options.B: Matter is made of tiny particles

questions.explanation: The particulate nature of matter means that all matter is made of extremely small particles.

questions.question: Which experiment demonstrates that matter can be broken into smaller particles?
questions.explanation: Grinding chalk into powder shows that matter can be divided into smaller particles.

questions.question: What happens when sugar dissolves in water?

options.B: Sugar particles mix with water particles

questions.explanation: Sugar dissolves because its particles mix uniformly with water particles.

questions.question: What do spaces between particles of matter indicate?

options.B: Particles have empty spaces between them

questions.explanation: Matter contains spaces between particles which allow substances to mix.

questions.question: What holds particles of matter together?
questions.explanation: Particles of matter are held together by forces of attraction.

questions.question: Which state of matter has particles that are tightly packed?
questions.explanation: In solids, particles are closely packed and strongly attracted.

questions.question: Which state of matter has definite volume but no definite shape?
questions.explanation: Liquids maintain a fixed volume but take the shape of their container.

questions.question: Which state of matter has particles that move freely in all directions?
questions.explanation: Gas particles move freely and occupy the entire container.

questions.question: Why can gases be compressed easily?

options.B: They have large spaces between particles

questions.explanation: Gas particles are far apart, allowing compression.

questions.question: Which device is used to demonstrate compressibility of gases?
questions.explanation: A syringe experiment demonstrates gas compressibility.

questions.question: What is diffusion?

options.A: Movement of particles from one place to another

questions.explanation: Diffusion is the spreading of particles due to constant motion.

questions.question: Which substance is used to demonstrate diffusion in water in experiments?
questions.explanation: Potassium permanganate crystals spread color in water due to diffusion.

questions.question: Diffusion occurs faster in which condition?
questions.explanation: Higher temperature increases particle movement and diffusion rate.

questions.question: What is the melting point?

options.A: Temperature at which solid changes to liquid

questions.explanation: Melting point is the temperature where a solid turns into liquid.

questions.question: At what temperature does ice melt?
questions.explanation: Ice melts at 0°C under normal atmospheric pressure.

questions.question: What is boiling point?
questions.explanation: Boiling point is the temperature at which a liquid changes into gas.

questions.question: Which process changes liquid slowly into vapour below boiling point?
questions.explanation: Evaporation occurs at the surface of a liquid below boiling temperature.

questions.question: Which state of matter can flow easily?
questions.explanation: Liquids and gases are fluids and can flow easily.

questions.question: Why does perfume smell spread across a room?

options.B: Due to diffusion of gas particles

questions.explanation: Perfume molecules diffuse in air and spread throughout the room.

questions.question: Which property allows gases to occupy the entire container?
questions.explanation: Gas particles move freely and spread throughout available space.

questions.question: Which state of matter has the strongest interparticle attraction?
questions.explanation: Particles in solids have the strongest forces of attraction.

questions.question: Which phenomenon explains why sugar spreads uniformly in water?
questions.explanation: Diffusion causes sugar particles to spread throughout water.

questions.question: Which property explains why gases are highly compressible?
questions.explanation: Large spaces between particles allow gases to compress.

questions.question: Which example demonstrates diffusion in gases?
questions.explanation: Perfume smell spreads due to diffusion of gas particles.

questions.question: Which concept explains why solids maintain their shape?
questions.explanation: Strong attractive forces keep particles fixed in solids.
